channel.title: Bali Culture on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Bali Culture on Bali Kini

item.title: Galungan 2026: The Complete Guide for Visitors and Locals
item.description: Galungan 2026 falls on June 17, 2026, followed by Kuningan on June 27, 2026. For the Balinese, this is the most sacred moment in the Hindu Balinese calendar — and for visitors, it is simply the best time to see Bali in its most authentic form. What Is Galungan? Galungan celebrates the victory of dharma (good) over adharma (evil). The festival occurs every 210 days according to the Balinese Pawukon calendar.

item.title: Subak: How Water Teaches Humanity About Unity and the Sacred
item.description: On a quiet morning in the highlands of Tabanan, an elderly farmer walks along the narrow dykes of his rice field. He is not simply checking his crop — he is fulfilling a duty to his community, to nature, and to God. The system that governs his life has existed for more than a thousand years. It is called Subak. More Than Just an Irrigation System Subak is often simplified as “Bali’s traditional irrigation system.
